checks.name: worker-startup-classification
checks.detail: Worker startup now classifies free ports, same-service reuse, different-HappyTG-service conflicts, and foreign HTTP listener conflicts without a raw Node EADDRINUSE stack trace.

checks.name: worker-runtime-side-effects
checks.detail: Worker runtime start moved behind successful bind, so same-service reuse does not start a second local maintenance loop.

checks.name: bot-startup-classification
checks.detail: Bot startup now follows the same reuse-vs-conflict model already implied by bootstrap and docs, replacing raw listen EADDRINUSE details with truthful action-oriented messaging.

checks.name: bot-no-duplicate-delivery-loop
checks.detail: Bot reuse short-circuits before Telegram polling or webhook inspection, so an already-running HappyTG Bot is reused rather than doubled.

checks.name: docs-truthfulness
checks.detail: Quickstart and troubleshooting now document occupied-port guidance for bot and worker alongside the existing API and Mini App guidance.

checks.name: repo-verification
checks.detail: Fresh repo-level lint, typecheck, test, and happytg verify completed after the fix; happytg verify only reported an unrelated existing Codex transport warning plus running-stack reuse info.
